title: Roureopsis fasciculata
taxonomy: Roureopsis fasciculata is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It is native to tropical Africa, particularly in countries such as Cameroon, Nigeria, and the Democratic Republic of the Congo. The classification of Roureopsis fasciculata places it in the genus Roureopsis, which is part of the subfamily Faboideae. This genus is closely related to other African legume species. Roureopsis fasciculata is also known by its synonym, Lonchocarpus fasciculatus.

intro: Roureopsis fasciculata, commonly known as African Bushwillow, is a deciduous tree native to Southern Africa, belonging to the family Combretaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimalarial, and antimicrobial effects, making it a valuable resource for treating various ailments. The bioactive constituents of Roureopsis fasciculata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
